div.h1: Calibration Dataset Size vs Accuracy Stability
div.p: Line graph showing accuracy variance convergence with increasing calibration samples

Key Insight: Calibration scales stabilize when the calibration dataset captures the true activation distribution. Below 100 samples, random outliers dominate. Above 500 samples, diminishing returns set in. The sweet spot is 100-500 representative samples.

g.text: Samples | Scale σ² | Accuracy Drop | Status
